name: Russ Columbo
abstract: Ruggiero Eugenio di Rodolfo Colombo (January 14, 1908 – September 2, 1934), known as Russ Columbo, was an American baritone, songwriter, violinist, and actor. He is famous for romantic ballads such as his signature tune "You Call It Madness, But I Call It Love" and his own compositions "Prisoner of Love" and "Too Beautiful for Words".

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: And then something else happened to the show that hadn't happened since Russ Columbo, the matinee idol, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: I also liked Russ Columbo. Russ Columbo was very handsome... He was, I thought, better than Bing Crosby
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: Bing Crosby had met this guy Russ Columbo... I know the first theatre date he had in New York was with...he was on the bill with us
